2026-04-23T03:50:25.4788536Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T03:50:25.4789128Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:25.4789564Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:25.4789956Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:25.4790258Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:25.4790492Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-23T03:53:19.6198565Z [2026-04-23T03:50:28Z] [DEBUG] [rpc] safeoutputs←resp 11883b {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"tools":[{"description":"Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. 2026-04-23T03:53:19.6738459Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T03:51:19.977130156Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58543,\"title\":\"[Consumption] Docs: Add code samples and improve key concepts for Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 5e2e0280c (PR #58536) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption` package (v1.1.0 → v1.2.0-beta.1) was added to the repository. The package exposes a rich API surface covering Azure cost-management operations: consumption budgets, usage details, marketplace purchases, charges, reservation summaries/details, and reservation recommendations. Despite this breadth, the README contains **no inline code examples** and the `tests/Samples/` directory does not exist.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- No service-specific code examples showing how to authenticate and call the main APIs (e.g., list usage details, manage budgets, retrieve charges)\\n- `tests/Samples/` directory is missing — no test-backed snippets to power the `Update-Snippets.ps1` workflow\\n- **Key concepts** section is a single generic link; it does not describe the Consumption resource model, scope-based access (`ArmClient` + `ResourceIdentifier` scope), or the main resource types (`ConsumptionBudgetResource`, `ConsumptionUsageDetail`, etc.)\\n- Examples section only references generic \\u0026#34;all Azure SDK samples\\u0026#34; links; no Consumption-specific walkthroughs\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the `## Key concepts` section with content that describes:\\n\\n- **Scope-based access**: All Consumption APIs accept a `ResourceIdentifier scope` (subscription, management group, billing account, etc.)\\n- **ConsumptionBudgetResource**: Represents a cost budget with alert thresholds\\n- **ConsumptionUsageDetail**: Represents individual usage line items\\n- **ConsumptionChargeSummary / ConsumptionMarketplace**: Charge and marketplace purchase summaries\\n- **Reservation APIs**: Summaries, details, and recommendations for reserved capacity\\n\\nReplace the `## Examples` section to reference snippet placeholders (see Step 2).\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/tests/Samples/Sample1_Budgets.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_Budgets\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateAndGetBudget()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateConsumptionBudget\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier scope = new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ConsumptionBudgetCollection budgets = client.GetConsumptionBudgets(scope);\\n\\n            // Create a monthly budget with a $100 limit\\n            string budgetName = \\u0026#34;myMonthlyBudget\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConsumptionBudgetData budgetData = new ConsumptionBudgetData\\n            {\\n                Category = BudgetCategory.Cost,\\n                Amount = 100,\\n                TimeGrain = BudgetTimeGrainType.Monthly,\\n                TimePeriod = new BudgetTimePeriod(\\n                    startDate: DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,\\n                    endDate: DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddYears(1))\\n            };\\n            ArmOperation createOp = await budgets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, budgetName, budgetData);\\n            ConsumptionBudgetResource budget = createOp.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Budget \\u0026#39;{budget.Data.Name}\\u0026#39; created with limit {budget.Data.Amount}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListBudgets()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListConsumptionBudgets\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier scope = new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ConsumptionBudgetResource budget in client.GetConsumptionBudgets(scope))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Budget: {budget.Data.Name}, Limit: {budget.Data.Amount}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/tests/Samples/Sample2_UsageDetails.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample2_UsageDetails\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListUsageDetails()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListConsumptionUsageDetails\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier scope = new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ConsumptionUsageDetail usageDetail in client.GetConsumptionUsageDetailsAsync(scope))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Resource: {usageDetail.Id}, Cost: {usageDetail.PretaxCost}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Add snippet placeholders** to `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/README.md` in the `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a budget\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List budgets\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List usage details\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently contains all required top-level sections. The following sections need improvement:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action needed |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with service-specific description of scope model and main resource types |\\n| Examples | ❌ No inline code | Add snippet placeholders (see Step 2) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 consumption` — injects snippet code from test `#region` tags into README placeholders; verify the diff shows the snippets populated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 consumption` — regenerates public API listing files (no public API was changed in this push, but run as a precaution)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-23T03:53:19.6812500Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T03:51:20.162257054Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58543,\"title\":\"[Consumption] Docs: Add code samples and improve key concepts for Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 5e2e0280c (PR #58536) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption` package (v1.1.0 → v1.2.0-beta.1) was added to the repository. The package exposes a rich API surface covering Azure cost-management operations: consumption budgets, usage details, marketplace purchases, charges, reservation summaries/details, and reservation recommendations. Despite this breadth, the README contains **no inline code examples** and the `tests/Samples/` directory does not exist.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- No service-specific code examples showing how to authenticate and call the main APIs (e.g., list usage details, manage budgets, retrieve charges)\\n- `tests/Samples/` directory is missing — no test-backed snippets to power the `Update-Snippets.ps1` workflow\\n- **Key concepts** section is a single generic link; it does not describe the Consumption resource model, scope-based access (`ArmClient` + `ResourceIdentifier` scope), or the main resource types (`ConsumptionBudgetResource`, `ConsumptionUsageDetail`, etc.)\\n- Examples section only references generic \\u0026#34;all Azure SDK samples\\u0026#34; links; no Consumption-specific walkthroughs\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the `## Key concepts` section with content that describes:\\n\\n- **Scope-based access**: All Consumption APIs accept a `ResourceIdentifier scope` (subscription, management group, billing account, etc.)\\n- **ConsumptionBudgetResource**: Represents a cost budget with alert thresholds\\n- **ConsumptionUsageDetail**: Represents individual usage line items\\n- **ConsumptionChargeSummary / ConsumptionMarketplace**: Charge and marketplace purchase summaries\\n- **Reservation APIs**: Summaries, details, and recommendations for reserved capacity\\n\\nReplace the `## Examples` section to reference snippet placeholders (see Step 2).\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/tests/Samples/Sample1_Budgets.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_Budgets\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateAndGetBudget()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateConsumptionBudget\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier scope = new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ConsumptionBudgetCollection budgets = client.GetConsumptionBudgets(scope);\\n\\n            // Create a monthly budget with a $100 limit\\n            string budgetName = \\u0026#34;myMonthlyBudget\\u0026#34;;\\n            ConsumptionBudgetData budgetData = new ConsumptionBudgetData\\n            {\\n                Category = BudgetCategory.Cost,\\n                Amount = 100,\\n                TimeGrain = BudgetTimeGrainType.Monthly,\\n                TimePeriod = new BudgetTimePeriod(\\n                    startDate: DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,\\n                    endDate: DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddYears(1))\\n            };\\n            ArmOperation createOp = await budgets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, budgetName, budgetData);\\n            ConsumptionBudgetResource budget = createOp.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Budget \\u0026#39;{budget.Data.Name}\\u0026#39; created with limit {budget.Data.Amount}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListBudgets()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListConsumptionBudgets\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier scope = new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ConsumptionBudgetResource budget in client.GetConsumptionBudgets(scope))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Budget: {budget.Data.Name}, Limit: {budget.Data.Amount}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/tests/Samples/Sample2_UsageDetails.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample2_UsageDetails\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListUsageDetails()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListConsumptionUsageDetails\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier scope = new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ConsumptionUsageDetail usageDetail in client.GetConsumptionUsageDetailsAsync(scope))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Resource: {usageDetail.Id}, Cost: {usageDetail.PretaxCost}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Add snippet placeholders** to `sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/README.md` in the `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a budget\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List budgets\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List usage details\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently contains all required top-level sections. The following sections need improvement:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action needed |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with service-specific description of scope model and main resource types |\\n| Examples | ❌ No inline code | Add snippet placeholders (see Step 2) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/consumption/Azure.ResourceManager.Consumption/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 consumption` — injects snippet code from test `#region` tags into README placeholders;
